<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.92) — files are tightly interconnected
[suggestion] [info] Consider renaming to 'prd-components' or 'prd-ui' to accurately reflect PRD-specific component responsibilities rather than middleware designation
[suggestion] [info] Extract generic UI components (copy-link-button, guide) to appropriate utility zone - these don't align with middleware responsibilities

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.92) — files are tightly interconnected
- High-quality zone architecture with excellent cohesion (0.92) and minimal coupling (0.08)
- Central to PRD workflows with 62 files covering tree operations, messaging, and workflow views
- Strong bidirectional integration with Dashboard Frontend suggests seamless user experience between shell and PRD features
- Outstanding architectural quality with 0.92 cohesion and 0.08 coupling demonstrates well-designed component boundaries
- Comprehensive PRD tree management including bulk actions, inline forms, and workflow automation enhances planning productivity
- Message coalescing and throttling infrastructure optimizes real-time UI updates and prevents performance degradation
- Maintains excellent internal cohesion (0.92) despite heavy bidirectional communication, indicating well-designed component boundaries
- Contains PRD tree components that are functionally related to components in web-utils zone
- Component distribution across web-middleware and web-utils suggests incomplete domain decomposition
- PRD tree components split between web-middleware and web-utils zones violates component cohesion - related UI components should be co-located
- Contains generic UI components (copy-link-button.ts, guide.ts) that semantically belong in utility zones rather than middleware layer
- Middleware designation conflicts with actual component responsibilities - most files are PRD-specific UI components, not request/response middleware
- Extract generic UI components (copy-link-button, guide) to appropriate utility zone - these don't align with middleware responsibilities
- Consider renaming to 'prd-components' or 'prd-ui' to accurately reflect PRD-specific component responsibilities rather than middleware designation
- [call graph] 1284 internal calls, 4 outgoing, 5 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
